title: The Way We Was

jokes.text: Do you have to sit so close? It'll hurt your eyes.
jokes.setup: Family watching TV together
jokes.punchline: Classic parental concern about sitting too close to TV
jokes.explanation: Standard parental line, works for character but very predictable

jokes.text: Our next movie is McBain... a shoot-them-up, splatter-fest from the Hollywood cookie cutter.
jokes.setup: Professional movie critic reviewing an action film
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of generic action movie tropes
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious film criticism and formulaic action movies

jokes.text: That makes two of us.
jokes.setup: McBain being fired from the force
jokes.punchline: Cool one-liner response to being kicked out
jokes.explanation: Classic action movie one-liner, well-executed parody of the genre

jokes.text: Are we talking about the same movie? McBain was a nonstop roller coaster of chills, thrills, spills and kills.
jokes.setup: Critic dismissed movie as formulaic
jokes.punchline: Bart's over-the-top positive review with rhyming alliteration
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mocking movie marketing speak, shows Bart's enthusiasm for mindless action

jokes.text: That movie stinks like your creepy apartment.
jokes.setup: Sibling argument about the movie
jokes.punchline: Lisa's cutting personal insult about Bart
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry dynamic, Lisa being unexpectedly harsh

jokes.text: I love watching the bald guy argue with the fat tub of lard.
jokes.setup: Kids arguing about a movie
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude description of his own children
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - crude but funny, treats his kids like entertainment

jokes.text: All right, all right. Time for Dr. TV to perform a little surgery!
jokes.setup: TV is malfunctioning, family wants Homer to fix it
jokes.punchline: Homer's grandiose self-designation as 'Dr. TV'
jokes.explanation: Homer's overconfidence about his repair skills, good character moment

jokes.text: Looks like you lost the patient, doc.
jokes.setup: Homer called himself 'Dr. TV' and presumably broke the TV worse
jokes.punchline: Bart continues the medical metaphor after Homer's failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends Homer's metaphor, well-timed after his failure

jokes.text: Shut up. Cheap Chinese TV, why'd I ever buy it?
jokes.setup: Homer failed to fix the TV
jokes.punchline: Blames the TV rather than his own incompetence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer deflection - never his fault, always blames external factors

jokes.text: You're staring at a dot.
jokes.setup: Family trying to watch broken TV, claiming they can see something
jokes.punchline: Lisa's blunt reality check
jokes.explanation: Lisa as voice of reason, deflating the family's self-deception

jokes.text: She's right! She's right! Oh, how I miss TV!
jokes.setup: Lisa points out they're staring at a dot
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate dramatic despair about missing TV
jokes.explanation: Homer's instant mood swing from denial to despair, very character-specific

jokes.text: Dear God, give me one channel!
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting the loss of TV
jokes.punchline: Desperate prayer for minimal TV access
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - treating TV loss as genuine crisis worthy of divine intervention

jokes.text: You lie!
jokes.setup: Marge claims they didn't watch much TV before marriage
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic accusation of lying
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to imagine life without TV, dramatic overreaction

jokes.text: I ain't loving it.
jokes.setup: Parents describing romantic pre-marriage activities
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt dismissal of parental romance
jokes.explanation: Kid's natural aversion to parents' romantic past, good character reaction

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Doctor congratulating on pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of dismay
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase in perfect context - maximum economy and character integration

jokes.text: Gross.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions she and Homer were high school seniors who'd never met
jokes.punchline: Lisa's disgusted reaction to the romance setup
jokes.explanation: Standard kid reaction to parents' romance, mild but in character

jokes.text: Who needs that? I'm never going to England. Let's smoke.
jokes.setup: Homer being told he's late for English class
jokes.punchline: Homer's logical fallacy that English class is only useful for going to England
jokes.explanation: Perfect young Homer logic - hilariously literal interpretation of 'English' class

jokes.text: Not if I have to do heavy lifting or math.
jokes.setup: Friend asks if Marge deserves to earn as much as a man for the same job
jokes.punchline: Marge's self-deprecating response about traditional gender limitations
jokes.explanation: Good period humor about 1970s gender attitudes, shows young Marge's conflicted views

jokes.text: Boy, you don't gain a pound. It's my metabolism. I'm one of the lucky ones.
jokes.setup: Barney commenting on Homer not gaining weight
jokes.punchline: Homer attributing his thinness to good metabolism (ironic knowing future Homer)
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - audience knows thin teenage Homer becomes fat adult Homer

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't Simpson and Gumble, Springfield's Cheech and Chong.
jokes.setup: Homer and Barney caught smoking/being delinquent
jokes.punchline: Principal comparing them to famous stoner comedy duo
jokes.explanation: Good period reference, characterizes Homer and Barney as school troublemakers

jokes.text: Yeah, mama! Take it off!
jokes.setup: Female speaker talking about freeing themselves from male-imposed shackles
jokes.punchline: Male student completely misunderstanding the feminist message
jokes.explanation: Good satire of teenage boys completely missing the point of feminism

jokes.text: I wouldn't go if you were Elliott Gould!
jokes.setup: Homer asking girl to prom
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific celebrity rejection reference
jokes.explanation: Random 70s celebrity reference creates period-specific humor

jokes.text: There's gotta be a girl nobody wants to take.
jokes.setup: Homer striking out with prom dates
jokes.punchline: Barney's brutally pragmatic dating advice
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny but realistic teenage desperation approach to dating

jokes.text: -Hey, would you like to go--? -She's mine!
jokes.setup: Homer and Barney both see Marge at the same time
jokes.punchline: Barney immediately claiming Marge before Homer can finish asking
jokes.explanation: Good timing on the interruption, shows competitive friendship dynamic

jokes.text: -So what are you in for? -I'm a political prisoner.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why Marge is in detention
jokes.punchline: Marge's dramatic self-description as political prisoner
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - young Marge's idealistic self-perception

jokes.text: Every day, I show up, act like me and they slap me in here.
jokes.setup: Discussion about why they're in detention
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest assessment that just being himself gets him in trouble
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness - his natural personality is inherently disruptive

jokes.text: -What? -Another day of detention! -Let's get together. -Two days! -What's your name? -Homer. -Three days! -J. Simpson. -Five! -It was worth it! Six days!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to talk to Marge in detention
jokes.punchline: Rapid escalation of detention days as Homer keeps talking
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy - each word makes it worse, shows Homer's romantic determination

jokes.text: -But, Dad, I don't drink-- -Cut the crap! 'I collect the cans.'
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he doesn't drink
jokes.punchline: Abe calling out Homer's obvious lie about collecting beer cans
jokes.explanation: Good father-son dynamic, Abe seeing through Homer's transparent excuse

jokes.text: Oh, son, don't overreach! Go for the dented car, the dead-end job, the less attractive girl!
jokes.setup: Homer asking for dating advice about a great girl
jokes.punchline: Abe's brutally pessimistic life advice
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Grampa wisdom - hilariously defeatist parenting approach

jokes.text: I met this girl, Marge, and I wanna force her to like me.
jokes.setup: Homer asking guidance counselor for help
jokes.punchline: Homer's hilariously inappropriate phrasing about romance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - not understanding how creepy 'force her to like me' sounds

jokes.text: Me? I'm gonna drink a lot of beer and stay out all night!
jokes.setup: Counselor asking about post-graduation plans
jokes.punchline: Homer's hilariously honest and irresponsible life plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage Homer honesty - no pretense about having ambitions

jokes.text: Me, in a nuclear power plant. Kaboom!
jokes.setup: Counselor suggesting Homer work at the new nuclear plant
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately imagining nuclear disaster
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - Homer correctly predicts his future incompetence at the plant

jokes.text: I'll take that, stinkpot! Just warming up, Mrs. Bloominstein.
jokes.setup: Homer joining debate team to get close to Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately being combative and calling teacher 'stinkpot'
jokes.explanation: Homer completely missing the point of civil debate, immediately antagonistic

jokes.text: Fifty-five? That's ridiculous! Sure, it'll save a few lives, but millions will be late!
jokes.setup: Debate topic about lowering speed limit to 55 mph
jokes.punchline: Homer's warped priorities valuing punctuality over human lives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - hilariously twisted value system prioritizing convenience over safety

jokes.text: -Ignoramus? -It means I'm stupid, doesn't it? -Stupidity is different. -Not to me.
jokes.setup: Marge calls Homer ignoramus during debate
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple equation of ignorance with stupidity
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to grasp nuanced distinctions, everything is black and white

jokes.text: Ay, carumba!
jokes.setup: Homer presumably does something outrageous during debate (likely moons audience)
jokes.punchline: Classic Bart catchphrase used by background character
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from anachronistic use of Bart's catchphrase in flashback

jokes.text: You may not remember me. I mooned for rebuttal.
jokes.setup: Homer approaching Marge after embarrassing debate performance
jokes.punchline: Homer's pun on 'moved for rebuttal' with his inappropriate behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that acknowledges Homer's inappropriate debate tactic while being unexpectedly witty

jokes.text: I'm not asking you to like me or let me touch your goodies. Just be fair.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Marge to give him a chance
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriately explicit clarification of his modest request
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness to appropriate conversation - mentions inappropriate things while claiming to be respectful

jokes.text: I think if he applies himself, trains real hard, he could go another foot.
jokes.setup: Homer's athletic reference describing his shot-put ability
jokes.punchline: Coach's hilariously minimal assessment of Homer's potential improvement
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor - coach's assessment is technically positive but reveals Homer's mediocrity

jokes.text: I had him for four years. Solid 'C' student. Made a lamp last year.
jokes.setup: Teacher giving Homer a reference
jokes.punchline: Teacher's underwhelming assessment topped by mentioning Homer's singular achievement
jokes.explanation: Homer's mediocrity highlighted by teacher's attempt to find something positive to say

jokes.text: He's all things to men and maybe to a lucky gal. Wanna go to prom with me?
jokes.setup: Barney giving Homer a glowing reference
jokes.punchline: Barney immediately pivoting to asking the girl to prom himself
jokes.explanation: Good betrayal moment - Barney sabotaging Homer while seemingly helping him

jokes.text: French? What a coincidence! Just the subject I'm having trouble with.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions she tutors French
jokes.punchline: Homer's obvious lie to get tutoring time with her
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy setup - transparent excuse to spend time together

jokes.text: -Why, you cagey old dog! -Great story. Work, damn you, work!
jokes.setup: Homer telling the story gets carried away praising his past self
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking from narration to fight with the broken TV
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Homer breaking character to return to present situation

jokes.text: Uh-oh, a zit. No problemo.
jokes.setup: Homer preparing for his tutoring date with Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual confidence about dealing with acne
jokes.explanation: Mild teenage preparation humor, setup for visual gag we can't fully see

jokes.text: The perfect crime.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably covering his pimple with makeup or concealer
jokes.punchline: Homer treating acne concealment as criminal mastermind behavior
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to overdramatize mundane activities, good character moment

jokes.text: -Why is there make-out music on? -It helps me study.
jokes.setup: Homer has romantic music playing during tutoring session
jokes.punchline: Homer's transparent excuse for the romantic atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Homer's obvious romantic agenda disguised as study aid

jokes.text: You're telling me new stuff and minutes later, it's still there.
jokes.setup: Homer marveling at learning French
jokes.punchline: Homer's amazement at basic memory function
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Homer's low intellectual expectations for himself

jokes.text: When I see you forming vowels and continents--
jokes.setup: Homer trying to compliment Marge romantically
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing 'consonants' with 'continents' in his romantic speech
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism - trying to be romantic but revealing his ignorance

jokes.text: You're dear and honest and open, without a trace of pretension.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she likes Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge praising Homer for being genuine while he's actively deceiving her
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - Marge praising Homer for honesty while he's lying about French class

jokes.text: My tux will have the widest lapels and the highest platform shoes.
jokes.setup: Homer planning elaborate prom preparations
jokes.punchline: Homer's description of hilariously over-the-top 1970s fashion
jokes.explanation: Good period humor about 70s fashion excess

jokes.text: I'm not in any French class. This was a brilliant scheme to get to know you.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to be honest with Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer confessing his deception while praising his own cleverness
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness - confessing deception while expecting praise for his 'brilliant' plan

jokes.text: Homer J. Simpson, I hate you!
jokes.setup: Homer confessing his deception about French tutoring
jokes.punchline: Marge's full-name dramatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic turn - Marge's justified anger at Homer's deception

jokes.text: I know this story doesn't end with you telling it to us.
jokes.setup: Homer's story reaching a low point where Marge hates him
jokes.punchline: Lisa's logical observation about the story's necessary outcome
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor - Lisa pointing out the obvious narrative requirement

jokes.text: They married, had kids and bought a cheap TV.
jokes.setup: Lisa's observation about the story's ending
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical summary of their parents' entire relationship
jokes.explanation: Bart reducing their parents' romance to mundane reality, brings story full circle

jokes.text: This may not be an appropriate forum for what I'm about to put forth... but would you go to the prom with me?
jokes.setup: Artie speaking formally at forensics competition
jokes.punchline: Artie using formal debate language to ask Marge to prom
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Artie's pretentious formal speaking style even for casual requests

jokes.text: -Couldn't we just use rouge for this? -Ladies pinch. Whores use rouge.
jokes.setup: Patty instructing Marge on prom preparation
jokes.punchline: Patty's harsh judgment about makeup vs natural methods
jokes.explanation: Classic Patty prudishness and harsh moral judgments about proper femininity

jokes.text: That's what you get when you don't put out.
jokes.setup: Patty commenting on Marge's dating situation
jokes.punchline: Patty's crude explanation for why Marge gets worse dates
jokes.explanation: Shocking frankness from Patty about sexual relationships, very inappropriate but in character

jokes.text: Wait a second. If he's your date, who are you?
jokes.setup: Artie introduced as Marge's prom date, Homer also present
jokes.punchline: Father's confused question about Homer's purpose
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment highlighting Homer's awkward third wheel status

jokes.text: I'm a spare. Excuse me.
jokes.setup: Father asking Homer who he is
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-deprecating but accurate assessment of his role
jokes.explanation: Homer's honest self-assessment with dignity intact despite awkward situation

jokes.text: Say 'Cheez Whiz.'
jokes.setup: Father taking prom photos
jokes.punchline: Period-appropriate substitute for 'say cheese' using processed food brand
jokes.explanation: Mild period humor, slightly amusing brand name substitution

jokes.text: I paid for this car, tuxedo and the two dinners! We're going to the prom!
jokes.setup: Homer being told Marge is going with Artie
jokes.punchline: Homer's stubborn insistence based on his financial investment
jokes.explanation: Homer's practical logic overriding social norms, determined not to waste money

jokes.text: Wait a second, is that a bong? You have asthma? All right, move along.
jokes.setup: Principal chaperoning prom, sees suspicious item
jokes.punchline: Principal accepting obvious lie about drug paraphernalia being medical equipment
jokes.explanation: Good authority figure obliviousness, principal easily fooled by transparent excuse

jokes.text: -I thought you dropped out. -You wish, Dondalinger. -That's Mr. Dondalinger.
jokes.setup: Principal surprised to see Homer at prom
jokes.punchline: Homer's disrespectful response followed by principal's weak authority assertion
jokes.explanation: Good student-authority dynamic, Homer's continued disrespect even at prom

jokes.text: Hello, classmates. Instead of voting for an athletic hero or pretty boy... you have elected me, your intellectual superior, as your king.
jokes.setup: Artie winning prom king
jokes.punchline: Artie's pompous and insulting acceptance speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - turning honor into opportunity to insult his classmates

jokes.text: Usually when I have a thought, there's others. Something says yes and no. This time, it's only yes.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his certainty about belonging with Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's surprisingly articulate description of his usual mental confusion vs current clarity
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly deep Homer moment - honest self-reflection about his mental processes

jokes.text: -No, it's okay. I'm broke. I'll walk. -Yeah, why spoil a perfect evening?
jokes.setup: Homer can't afford to keep limo
jokes.punchline: Driver's sarcastic comment about the obviously disastrous evening
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm about the obviously terrible prom night experience

jokes.text: All right! All right! I'll walk in the mud!
jokes.setup: Homer arguing about road position
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic capitulation to walking in mud
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical overdramatic response to minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Once you stop this car, I'm gonna hug you and kiss you... and I'll never let you go.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge in car after reconciling
jokes.punchline: Homer's romantic declaration with slightly obsessive undertone
jokes.explanation: Sweet but slightly possessive Homer romantic moment, typical of his character

jokes.text: And I never have.
jokes.setup: Young Homer promising to never let Marge go
jokes.punchline: Present Homer confirming he kept that promise
jokes.explanation: Sweet completion of the romantic story, bringing it back to present

tmdb_overview: When the TV breaks, Marge and Homer entertain the kids with the story of how they first met.
insight: Flashback-heavy setup delivers 69 jokes across 22 minutes but feels scattered without standout hits.
